examples.title: Randomized Quicksort Algorithm
examples.description: Complete pseudocode for Randomized Quicksort with partition
examples.code:
RANDOMIZED-QUICKSORT(A, p, r)
BEGIN
    IF p < r THEN
        q = RANDOMIZED-PARTITION(A, p, r)
        RANDOMIZED-QUICKSORT(A, p, q - 1)
        RANDOMIZED-QUICKSORT(A, q + 1, r)
    END IF
END

RANDOMIZED-PARTITION(A, p, r)
BEGIN
    // Random pivot selection
    i = RANDOM(p, r)
    SWAP A[r], A[i]    // Swap pivot with last element
    
    // Lomuto partition scheme
    x = A[r]           // Pivot value
    i = p - 1
    
    FOR j = p TO r - 1 DO
        IF A[j] <= x THEN
            i = i + 1
            SWAP A[i], A[j]
        END IF
    END FOR
    
    SWAP A[i + 1], A[r]
    RETURN i + 1
END
examples.explanation: The algorithm uses random pivot selection to avoid worst-case. First, a random index is chosen and swapped with the last element. Then Lomuto partition divides elements into two parts: less than or equal to pivot, and greater than pivot. The pivot is placed in its correct position and returned.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n log n) expected, O(n²) worst-case
examples.spaceComplexity: O(log n) average for recursion stack

examples.title: Randomized Quicksort in Python
examples.description: Python implementation with tail recursion optimization
examples.code:
import random

def randomized_quicksort(arr, low, high):
    """Randomized Quicksort with tail recursion optimization"""
    while low < high:
        # Partition and get pivot index
        pi = randomized_partition(arr, low, high)
        
        # Optimize: recurse on smaller partition first
        if pi - low < high - pi:
            randomized_quicksort(arr, low, pi - 1)
            low = pi + 1  # Convert to iteration
        else:
            randomized_quicksort(arr, pi + 1, high)
            high = pi - 1  # Convert to iteration

def randomized_partition(arr, low, high):
    """Random pivot selection with Lomuto partition"""
    # Choose random pivot and swap with last element
    pivot_idx = random.randint(low, high)
    arr[pivot_idx], arr[high] = arr[high], arr[pivot_idx]
    
    pivot = arr[high]
    i = low - 1
    
    for j in range(low, high):
        if arr[j] <= pivot:
            i += 1
            arr[i], arr[j] = arr[j], arr[i]
    
    arr[i + 1], arr[high] = arr[high], arr[i + 1]
    return i + 1

# Example usage
arr = [10, 7, 8, 9, 1, 5]
randomized_quicksort(arr, 0, len(arr) - 1)
print("Sorted array:", arr)
examples.explanation: Python implementation includes tail recursion optimization to reduce stack space. The smaller partition is handled recursively while the larger one is converted to iteration. Uses random.randint for pivot selection and implements Lomuto partition scheme.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n log n) expected, O(n²) worst-case

examples.title: Three-Way Partition Randomized Quicksort
examples.description: C implementation with Dutch National Flag algorithm for duplicates
examples.explanation: Three-way partitioning (Dutch National Flag algorithm) efficiently handles duplicate elements. It creates three partitions: elements less than pivot, equal to pivot, and greater than pivot. This ensures O(n) time when all elements are equal, compared to O(n²) for standard partition.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n log n) average, O(n) for arrays with many duplicates

examples.title: Introsort Implementation
examples.description: Java implementation of Introsort combining Quicksort and Heapsort
examples.explanation: Introsort is a hybrid algorithm that starts with Quicksort and switches to Heapsort when recursion depth exceeds a threshold (2*log n). It also uses Insertion Sort for small subarrays. This guarantees O(n log n) worst-case time while maintaining Quicksort's average performance.
